title: Finite Linear Discriminant Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite linear-discriminant bad direction QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational Fisher-discriminant replay computes direction wy = 3/2 from S_w w = mu_B - mu_A, while a malformed row claims wy = 1, isolating a compact two-equation Farkas conflict.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional discriminant/classification rows only when they add distinct scatter, threshold, multiclass, regularized, probabilistic, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep statistical generalization and floating-point classifier claims in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational class samples, means, centered rows, within-class scatter matrices, Fisher direction, projected scores, threshold, margins, and ratio as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false discriminant-direction claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting direction.
Expose the pack through statistics, matrix computation, optimization, exact-vs-floating, discriminant/classification, and learner-query surfaces.

Keep Gaussian generative assumptions, Bayes optimality, multiclass or regularized LDA theory, statistical generalization, and floating-point covariance/classifier behavior marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
